description: High-level parallel state graphs for Project Skyjoust. Designed for a Rust + Bevy entity-component system (ECS) simulation with a fixed tick, event bus, and explicit query contracts between graphs.

execution_model.render_audio_ui: variable-rate presentation pass; never mutates authoritative simulation state
statechart_semantics: All graphs listed under root_parallel.regions run concurrently while their activation guard is true.
Transitions are driven by immutable events on the event bus or by pure guards evaluated during fixed ticks.
Guards are selectors over ECS resources/components; they do not mutate state.
Actions may issue ECS commands, mutate resources, and publish events, but should not read wall-clock time.
All multi-entity resolution uses stable entity ordering to preserve near-deterministic behaviour.

recommended_bevy_mapping.statecharts: resources that own graph state and current node path
recommended_bevy_mapping.events: Bevy Events or a custom deterministic ring-buffer event bus
recommended_bevy_mapping.guards: pure query functions over World snapshots
recommended_bevy_mapping.actions: systems that consume transition commands after graph evaluation

event.truce_active_between_teams.expression: team_pair in CampaignState.active_truces and MatchRules.friendly_fire == false

player.has_altitude_advantage.expression: player.y < opponent.y - altitude_threshold_px and player.vy <= opponent.vy + dive_bias

player_actor.scope: one instance per controllable rider entity

event_routes.notes: Primary arcade-skill event; contains winner, loser, outcome, angle, speed, altitude, brace_quality.

laurels.validation: grant only after tournament or event completion
